stories.title: Anthropic Mythos AI: The Model That Could Hack Anything — Now in Safe Mode
high_priority_fixes: No OG meta tags, Twitter cards, or meta description in HTML — only a META comment block. Add <meta property="og:title">, <meta name="description">, <meta name="twitter:card"> tags to the <head>.
Does not link editorial.css v4 — uses inline <style> with duplicate custom CSS, increasing page weight by ~3KB and bypassing the design system.

Uses v2/v3 class names: .stats-bar, .pull-quote, .timeline, .timeline-item, .date, .event, .sources-box, .bottom-line, .chart-container instead of v4 equivalents (.stat-grid, .story-quote, .story-timeline, .story-timeline-item, .tl-date, .tl-title, .story-sources, .story-chart, .story-chart-caption).
No confidence badges or evidence cards — claims like '10,000+ vulnerabilities' lack disclosed confidence levels.
Hero image uses custom .hero-image class with inline background-image (which gets stripped by template transformContent) instead of .story-hero-media > img pattern.
No .article-body wrapper — content is raw HTML that won't receive editorial.css body styling.
Uses .stat-number directly on <div> elements instead of the v4 pattern .stat-card .stat-number (stat-number is only valid as a child of .stat-card in v4).
No .story-sources class — uses .sources-box which is a v2/v3 class.
medium_priority_fixes: Uses 'Bebas Neue' font family in inline CSS but never imports it via @font-face or Google Fonts — will fall back to system sans-serif.
Uses .bottom-line which has no v4 equivalent — should use .story-callout.key-insight or .evidence-card instead.
Uses .model-grid and .model-card custom classes — consider using .story-comparison v4 pattern for side-by-side comparisons.
No byline avatar or author credit beyond 'The Breakdown' — consider adding named journalist credit per editorial OS standards.
No Reading Estimate or Intel Summary section (provided by template in JS mode, but missing from standalone view).
Related links use custom .related-links class instead of .story-related / .story-related-card pattern.
Timeline items lack .tl-detail class — use .tl-detail for detail text instead of just .event (which maps to .tl-title).

stories.title: Canada Bill C-34: The Safe Social Media Act — Under-16 Ban and AI Chatbot Regulation
high_priority_fixes: No OG meta tags, Twitter cards, meta description, or any <meta> tags at all — no META comment block either. SEO score is critically low.
No hero image — story opens with a stats-bar. Add a .story-hero-media > img hero image per v4 pattern.
Does not link editorial.css v4 — uses inline <style> with ~1.8KB of custom CSS duplicating design system styles.
Uses v2/v3 class names: .stats-bar, .pull-quote, .chart-container, .sources-box, .bottom-line instead of v4 equivalents.
No confidence badges or evidence cards — claims about '5M teen accounts deactivated' lack disclosed confidence level.
No .article-body wrapper — content is raw HTML without editorial.css body styling.
Uses .stat-number directly on <div> elements instead of v4 .stat-card .stat-number pattern.
No .story-sources class — uses .sources-box which is a v2/v3 class.
medium_priority_fixes: No section divider or section-number classes — consider using .section-divider / .section-number for major sections.
No timeline component — the global context section would benefit from a .story-timeline.
No byline, author credit, or publication date visible in the standalone HTML.
Uses inline SVG-like images hosted on Supabase (.chart-container img) which could break if storage URL changes.
Uses .bottom-line which has no v4 equivalent — should use .story-callout.key-insight.
No intel-summary section with 'What happened / Why it matters' panel.
No sticky nav or table of contents — story is 102 lines but lacks navigational aids.

Hero image uses custom .hero-image class with inline background (stripped by template transformContent) instead of .story-hero-media > img pattern.
No confidence badges or evidence cards — spec claims like 'Co-designed with Huawei (unconfirmed)' need explicit confidence disclosure.

stories.title: The Long Thaw: India and China Inch Toward Normalisation
high_priority_fixes: No OG meta tags, Twitter cards, meta description, or any <meta> tags at all — critical SEO failure.

No formal sources section — sources are only noted inline in SVG captions or as 'Source: MEA press releases, Xinhua, research briefs'. Must add a proper .story-sources / ol > li list with numbered sources per editorial OS standards.
Does not link editorial.css v4 — uses inline <style> with ~3KB of custom CSS duplicating design system styles.
No hero image using .story-hero-media > img pattern — uses custom .hero-image-wrapper with tag (gets stripped by template transformContent).
Uses fully custom HTML structure (.content-wrapper, .story-headline, .story-body, .winners-losers, .wl-card, .highlight-box, etc.) with no v4 class equivalents for most components.
No .article-body wrapper — content lacks editorial.css body styling.
No confidence badges or evidence cards — claims about trade deficit, troop numbers lack disclosed confidence levels.
Anonymous expert quotes at lines 223-231 — 'on condition of anonymity' needs Editor-in-Chief justification per editorial OS §4.5.
Uses CSS custom properties (--gold) in inline <style> that may conflict with :root tokens from editorial.css.
medium_priority_fixes: No byline or author credit — uses '.byline THE BREAKDOWN | June 24, 2026' as text, not structured HTML.
Stat cards use .num / .label instead of .stat-number / .stat-label per v4 pattern.
Uses custom .stat-grid that mimics v4 name but has different styling than .stat-grid from editorial.css.
Uses .bottom-line which has no v4 equivalent — should use .story-callout.key-insight.
No timeline component — uses SVG-based timeline in chart-container which is excellent but not semantic; consider adding a .story-timeline for accessibility.
No intel-summary or sticky nav for standalone viewing.
Winners/Losers cards use custom .wl-card — consider .story-comparison v4 pattern.
Expert quote boxes use custom .expert-quote — consider .evidence-card or .story-quote patterns.

stories.title: Europe Heatwave 2026 — France Hottest Day Ever, UK Breaches June Record, 40 Drownings
high_priority_fixes: No OG meta tags or Twitter cards in HTML — only a META comment block. Add actual <meta property="og:..."> tags.
Does not link editorial.css v4 — uses inline <style> with ~2.2KB of custom CSS duplicating design system styles.
Hero image uses custom .hero-image class with inline background (stripped by template transformContent) instead of .story-hero-media > img pattern.
Uses v2/v3 class names: .stats-bar, .pull-quote, .timeline, .timeline-item, .date, .event, .sources-box, .bottom-line, .chart-container, .related-links instead of v4 equivalents.
No .article-body wrapper — content is raw HTML without editorial.css body styling.
No confidence badges or evidence cards — temperature records and attribution data need confidence disclosure.
Uses .stat-number directly on <div> elements instead of v4 .stat-card .stat-number pattern.
No .story-sources class — uses .sources-box which is a v2/v3 class.
medium_priority_fixes: Uses custom .country-box classes for per-country stats — consider using .story-callout or .evidence-card patterns.
No byline avatar or structured author credit.
No intel-summary or sticky nav for standalone viewing.
Related links point to unrelated stories (AI Governance, US-Iran Peace) rather than climate-related content.
Timeline items use custom styling with red indicators (.E85050) — timeline should use .story-timeline-item with appropriate coloring.
Uses .bottom-line with red border (border:1px solid #E85050) — should use .story-callout.warning or appropriate variant.
Climameter attribution chart uses custom SVG instead of .story-chart pattern (though SVGs render fine, naming matters for transformContent).
No Reading Estimate or word count metadata in standalone view.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: No story links to editorial.css v4 — all 7 stories use inline <style> blocks with duplicate CSS, bypassing the design system entirely.
cross_cutting_issues.recommendation: Remove all inline <style> blocks and use only classes from editorial.css v4. The design system provides .stat-grid, .story-quote, .story-timeline, .story-sources, .story-chart, .story-callout, .evidence-card, .story-comparison, etc.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: No actual OG meta tags or Twitter cards in any story HTML — 4 stories have zero meta tags at all, 3 have only META comment blocks (not rendered HTML).
cross_cutting_issues.recommendation: Add <meta property="og:title">, <meta property="og:description">, <meta property="og:image">, <meta property="og:type" content="article">, <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image"> to every story.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: No story uses the v4 hero image pattern (.story-hero-media > img) — they use custom implementations that are stripped by the JS template's transformContent function.
cross_cutting_issues.recommendation:
If standalone: use
...
. If template-based: remove inline hero markup entirely (template strips it anyway).

cross_cutting_issues.issue: No story uses confidence badges (.confidence-high, .confidence-medium, .confidence-low) or evidence cards (.evidence-card) — violating editorial OS §3.2 Stage 5 which requires every claim to have a confidence level.
cross_cutting_issues.recommendation: Add .evidence-card with .confidence-badge for key claims. Use .confidence-high (green) for well-sourced claims, .confidence-medium (amber) for moderate confidence, .confidence-low (red) for limited evidence.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: No story wraps content in .article-body — all output raw HTML that won't receive editorial.css body text styling (paragraphs, links, headings).
cross_cutting_issues.recommendation:
Wrap story content in
...
.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: All stories use .stat-number as a top-level class on <div> elements instead of the v4 pattern where .stat-number is a child of .stat-card.
cross_cutting_issues.recommendation:
Use
...
...
with parent .stat-grid.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: All stories with sources use .sources-box instead of .story-sources v4 class.
cross_cutting_issues.recommendation: Replace .sources-box with .story-sources class. Keep the ol > li structure as-is (it matches v4 pattern).

cross_cutting_issues.issue: Timeline implementations use .timeline-item / .date / .event classes which map via transformContent to .story-timeline-item / .tl-date / .tl-title, but none use .tl-detail for description text.
cross_cutting_issues.recommendation:
Add .tl-detail class to the event description paragraph in each timeline item:
...
...
...
.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: Multiple stories use 'Bebas Neue' font family in inline CSS without importing it via @font-face or <link> — will silently fall back to system sans-serif.
cross_cutting_issues.recommendation: Add Google Fonts link for Bebas Neue in <head>, or replace with v4 heading font stack (Cormorant Garamond) from editorial.css.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: No story includes AI disclosure per editorial OS §5.4 — stories should state whether AI was used in research or drafting.
cross_cutting_issues.recommendation: Add an AI Disclosure section at the bottom of each story per OS §5.4 template.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: No story includes a composite confidence score (CCS) per editorial OS §3.2 Stage 7 — which requires percentage of claims rated High or Medium.
cross_cutting_issues.recommendation: Add '## Methodology & Sources' section with Composite Confidence Score, primary/secondary source counts, and last updated date per OS §3.2.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: doval-wang story uses anonymous sources ('on condition of anonymity') without Editor-in-Chief disclosure or justification for anonymity per editorial OS §4.5.
cross_cutting_issues.recommendation: Add justification for anonymous sourcing per OS §4.5: explain why anonymity is needed without identifying the source.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: Multiple stories use .bottom-line which has no v4 equivalent class in editorial.css — should use .story-callout.key-insight.
cross_cutting_issues.recommendation:
Replace .bottom-line with

The Bottom Line

...

.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: No story includes the intel-summary component (What happened / Why it matters / Key takeaway) for standalone readers.
cross_cutting_issues.recommendation:
Add
...
after the hero section for standalone builds.

cross_cutting_issues.issue: No story has a canonical URL tag or JSON-LD structured data (NewsArticle schema) for search engines.
cross_cutting_issues.recommendation: Add <link rel="canonical" href="..."> and <script type="application/ld+json"> with NewsArticle schema.
